title: Changes Made to the Inward Processing Circular Related to Machinery, Automotive, Other Transportation Vehicles, Electrical and Electronics Sectors
bodyHtml:
Changes have been made to the Circular that determines the application procedures and principles of Inward Processing Regime measures concerning the export and deemed export of machinery, automotive, other transportation vehicles, electrical, and electronics sector products.

The definition of the "Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) System" has been added to the Circular.

For companies holding an Authorized Economic Operator certificate, in the closure procedures of the Inward Processing Certificate (IPC), an Expert Report taken under the provisions of the current legislation or a "detailed sworn financial advisor report prepared based on Enterprise Resource Planning System Records (ERP)" can be used to determine and certify the consumption rates (including wastage and secondary processed goods rates) of goods imported or used as equivalent goods under the IPC in the production of goods exported or delivered domestically.

The Resource Planning System used by the company, which forms the basis for the preparation of the sworn financial advisor report, must:

-Be capable of determining the consumption rates, including wastage and secondary processed goods,

-Show the consumption rates of goods imported or used as equivalent goods under the IPC in the production of goods exported or delivered domestically (including wastage and secondary processed goods rates),

-Demonstrate the relationship between the imported goods subject to the IPC and the declarations or relevant invoices related to the export of the product, with detailed and clear explanations.

The changes related to the ERP in the Circular will also apply to certificates that are in open and closure request status as of May 14, 2024.

To access the updated version of the relevant Circular, click here.
